differentials.name: Enlargement + rupture irrespective of size
differentials.raw: Enlargement + rupture irrespective of size (lower rate than in noninflammatory aneurysm)

context: =  aortic wall no longer fully intact with hemorrhage enclosed by a thrombus and/or retroperitoneal soft tissues Angio: √  absent parenchymal stain = avascular halo √  displacement + stretching of aortic branches CT: √  loss of periaortic fat planes: √  commonly posterolateral focal area of soft-tissue attenuation intimately associated with aorta √  "draped aorta" sign = posterior aortic wall not identifiable as distinct from adjacent structures / closely apposed to adjacent vertebral body contour √  new saccular outpouching (= focal breach) of aneurysm wall in the region that appears draped o
